Collisions.title: Collisions Menu
Collisions.dataSource: Collision data provided by CelesTrak via {link}.

help.overview: The Collisions menu lists upcoming close approaches (conjunctions) between cataloged objects, using CelesTrak's SOCRATES conjunction assessment data. Each row is a predicted event with its probability and geometry, and clicking a row takes you straight to it in the simulation.
help.imgAlt: The Collisions menu showing a table of upcoming conjunctions
help.imgCaption: Upcoming conjunctions from SOCRATES, sorted with the riskiest events visible at a glance.
help.columns:
• TOCA: Time of Closest Approach (UTC).
  • #1 / #2: the NORAD IDs of the two objects involved.
  • Max Prob: the maximum estimated collision probability.
  • Min Range (km): predicted miss distance at closest approach.
  • Rel Speed (km/s): how fast the objects pass each other.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu and click the download icon to fetch the latest SOCRATES data (requires an internet connection).
  2. Click any row: the simulation time jumps to 30 seconds before closest approach and the first object is selected, with both IDs loaded into the search bar.
  3. Watch the conjunction unfold on the globe, or pause and step through it with the time controls.
  4. Use the refresh icon to pull updated data later.
help.tip1: SOCRATES data is updated periodically by CelesTrak. It is a screening product, not a real-time warning system.
help.tip2: After clicking a row, both objects are in the search bar; click the second one in the results to inspect it or set it as a secondary object.
help.tip3: The simulation jumps to 30 seconds before the event so you can watch the approach rather than the aftermath.
